key_insight: HVAF-sprayed Tribaloy-400 forms a denser, more protective oxide layer than detonation-sprayed coating, giving better resistance to molten solar-salt corrosion at 500 �b0C.
engineering_relevance: For concentrated solar power thermal storage containers, HVAF T-400 coatings appear more durable for long-duration high-temperature salt exposure than DS coatings.
method: Tribaloy-400 was deposited on ASTM A516 steel using HVAF and detonation spraying.
Samples were exposed to 60% NaNO3-40% KNO3 molten solar salt at 500 �b0C for up to 1000 h.
Phase, SEM, and elemental mapping were used to compare oxidation, salt ingress, and spallation behavior.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that HVAF retained a uniform Cr-Mo-Si-O-enriched surface with minimal salt penetration, while DS showed deeper ingress, higher mass gain, and more spallation after 1000 hours.
watch_item: The result is specific to the tested salt chemistry, temperature, substrate, and exposure time, so broader CSP service conditions still need validation.
paper_title: Hot Corrosion Behavior of Thermal Sprayed Using HVAF and DS Techniques with Tribaloy-400 Coatings for Solar Energy Storage Container
